summary: InstantMed presents a highly credible, beautifully structured mobile landing page with exceptional copy and robust trust positioning. The design borrows successfully from premium direct-to-consumer health services, but is held back by minor typographic contrast issues and layout inconsistencies with the pricing selector and the sticky CTA.

brand_spine.observation: The 'telehealth without the small talk' message lands perfectly within the first 5 seconds. Trust is established instantly via clean partner logos and an elegant certificate preview card.

findings.issue: The claimed delivery time on the comparison list (~20 min) contradicts the live ticker display in the sticky footer (~44 min).
findings.recommendation: Unify the messaging or explicitly label the comparison speed as 'from form submission to review' versus 'overall turnaround'.

typography.observation: Plus Jakarta Sans and Source Sans 3 are set with clean leading and letter-spacing, but tiny metadata text at the bottom is almost illegible.

findings.issue: The text in the sticky footer ticker is too small and lacks sufficient contrast against the blue background.
findings.recommendation: Increase font size of the ticker text to at least 11px and use a high-contrast white rather than muted gray.

color_and_surface.observation: The warm ivory background (#F8F7F4) paired with crisp white cards creates a calming, clinical-yet-modern aesthetic with proper visual restraint.

findings.issue: Compliance logos in the gray footer block have low contrast, hurting credibility.
findings.recommendation: Adjust the background of the partner logo strip or increase the opacity of the ADHA and AHPRA SVG assets.

motion.observation: The sidebar menu lacks premium fluid easing and feels mechanical, sliding out abruptly without a pleasant spring behavior.

findings.issue: The sidebar menu transition clips on initial frame trigger and lacks dynamic easing.
findings.recommendation: Replace basic CSS transition with a custom bezier transition: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) over 320ms.

copy_voice.observation: The clinical tone is exceptional, direct, and completely devoid of hype words, communicating clarity and ease of access immediately.

findings.issue: Meta-commentary ('Most patients are trying to avoid a waiting room, not decode a health platform') feels slightly unprofessional for a medical clinic context.
findings.recommendation: Change heading to focus strictly on patient benefits: 'Straightforward fees, zero surprises.'

hierarchy_and_layout.observation: The scroll rhythm flows logically, but there is visual clutter surrounding the multi-day certificate selector block on a 375px viewport.

findings.issue: The 'How many days' card selector creates a redundant block of UI right next to another start CTA.
findings.recommendation: Simplify the multi-day selection to a clean segmented control directly above a single unified start button.

conversion_friction.observation: The pricing is anchored early and next-step actions are obvious, making it incredibly fast to start a request.

findings.issue: Selecting a 2 or 3-day certificate ($29.95/$39.95) does not update the price listed in the sticky CTA footer ($19.95).
findings.recommendation: Bind the price state of the sticky footer button to the user's active day selector option on the page.

signature_devices.observation: Excellent implementation of the signature live-wait counter, real doctor signature element, and 'what we won't do' link in the navigation menu.

findings.issue: The live counter device is obscured inside the crowded sticky ticker instead of taking pride of place in the hero.
findings.recommendation: Place the live wait-time metric directly underneath the hero CTA button as a high-visibility micro-copy badge.

top_three_actions.why: Currently, selecting 2 or 3 days on the page ($29.95 or $39.95) doesn't update the sticky CTA at the bottom which remains hardcoded at $19.95, creating a high-severity checkout discrepancy.

top_three_actions.action: Elevate and style the live wait-time counter
top_three_actions.why: The signature 'Avg response: ~44 min' metric is currently buried in unreadable low-contrast text in the sticky footer instead of driving conversion confidence in the hero.
The current sidebar menu slide-out feels abrupt and mechanically raw, detracting from an otherwise high-end, premium feel.
